reasoning: There is currently no dedicated vaccine for gonorrhea, though the meningitis B vaccine (4CMenB) is being used off-label in the UK as of August 2025 to offer cross-protection. Despite significant media coverage of the UK rollout in August, the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page was not updated to reflect a 'Yes' for a gonorrhea vaccine throughout September and October. Given that dedicated vaccines (like GSK's) are still in early/mid-clinical trials and will take years to develop, and because Wikipedia editors have not classified the off-label MenB vaccine as a gonorrhea vaccine thus far, it is highly unlikely they will update the list page in the short span between November 2025 and February 2026. Therefore, probabilities are kept very close to the minimum, increasing slightly for later dates to account for baseline random edits.
